title: Goldman Sachs Raises Copper Price Target to $12,000/t on AI Infrastructure Supercycle
description: Goldman Sachs Raises Copper Price Target to $12,000/t on AI Infrastructure Supercycle Mining Weekly
htmlContent:
Goldman Sachs Raises Copper Price Target to $12,000/t on AI Infrastructure Supercycle

Creamer Media Reporter • 4th June 2026

JOHANNESBURG – Goldman Sachs has raised its 12-month copper price target to $12,000 per tonne, up from the previous forecast of $11,200, as the US investment bank's commodities research team declared that an "AI infrastructure supercycle" is reshaping the outlook for base metal demand.

In a note to institutional clients published on Wednesday, Goldman analysts argued that the simultaneous convergence of artificial intelligence compute buildout, electrification of transport and heating, and grid modernisation programmes across developed and emerging markets would absorb every available tonne of new copper supply through at least 2030.

"We are witnessing a demand inflection that is structurally different from any prior commodity cycle," the note read. "The AI buildout alone is adding a demand layer equivalent to several new Chinas, without a corresponding expansion in mine supply."

The bank estimated that global copper demand could exceed 30 million tonnes per year by 2028, compared to current annual production of roughly 22.5 million tonnes. Closing that gap would require the equivalent of 15 to 20 large new copper mines – a target that the industry is unlikely to meet given the 15-to-20-year timeline for greenfield project development.

Shares in major diversified miners responded positively. BHP rose 3.5%, Rio Tinto gained 2.8%, and Freeport-McMoRan – the world's largest listed copper producer – added 4.1% on the New York Stock Exchange following the note's publication.

Junior copper developers also attracted strong buying. Several exploration-stage companies with projects in Chile, Peru, the Democratic Republic of Congo and Zambia posted gains of between 5% and 12% on volumes well above their 30-day averages.

The Goldman upgrade follows similar upward revisions from Bank of America and Citigroup, both of which cited AI-driven demand in their most recent commodity outlooks. The consensus view across major investment banks now places 12-month copper above $11,500 per tonne for the first time in history.

Mining industry body the International Copper Association welcomed the outlook, noting that the "energy transition and digital revolution are moving in lockstep, and copper is at the centre of both."

Tags: Goldman Sachs, copper, price target, AI, supercycle, mining, BHP, Rio Tinto, Freeport

Sentiment signal: bullish
